findings.evidence: New lines 45-47 define `trivialTensor` exactly by `Comodule.trivial` and `of`; existing `code/TauCeti/Algebra/Coalgebra/Comodule/Trivial.lean:214:def trivial` and `code/TauCeti/Algebra/Coalgebra/Comodule/Finite.lean:95:abbrev of` already provide those pieces.
findings.fix: Remove `trivialTensor`; use `letI : Comodule R C (M ⊗[R] N) := Comodule.trivial ...; FGComoduleCat.of (R := R) (C := C) (M ⊗[R] N)` at call sites, or wait for the actual monoidal tensor object API.
findings.issue: `FGComoduleCat.trivialTensor` is just `FGComoduleCat.of` applied to `M ⊗[R] N` with the existing `Comodule.trivial`, so it creates a parallel tensor-named API without adding tensor-product structure.

summary: The remaining tensor-product API is only a renamed specialization of existing trivial-comodule and finite-bundling constructors. The PR should avoid adding a parallel `trivialTensor` object until there is real tensor-product structure to expose.
